scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B) events — both PRs explicitly alter the `events` table (`webhook_status`/`delivery_attempts` in Alan’s PR and `delivery_state`/`processor_version` in Sarah’s PR).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text:
C) Sarah — she only works Monday, Wednesday, Friday.  
Thursday March 13 is a Thursday, so Sarah is unavailable by her normal part-time schedule.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B) SAML SSO — Josh told Acme Corp they'd get it after the auth migration, but Alan explicitly said SAML was not in this phase and would add 3 weeks.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new flow removed 4 steps, including **first milestone**, which used to be the activation trigger. So “completion” now means something different, making 60% vs 85% not apples-to-apples.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. It was created for “load testing” but the workspace says it was never actually used for load testing.

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text:
B) All three were in migration batch-7, run on February 25.  
The tickets share different symptoms, but each customer record explicitly says “Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25,” which points to a common migration-related root cause.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: C — Only the webhook payload format remains open. The workspace says cursor vs offset pagination has consensus: “Sarah recommends cursor, Alan agrees,” and the design doc summary lists just 2 open questions with pagination effectively settled and webhook still debated.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B) `src/billing/mapping.rs` — Alan calls it the “rosetta stone file,” and it maps between `plan_id`, `tier_id`, and `subscription`.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B) CSV only — all 3 customers confirmed CSV is sufficient; PDF was never requested and Excel came from the sales rep, not the customer.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C) 0 edge case tests and 7 e2e tests — the suite is 82% unit tests and 16% integration tests, with **0 edge case tests** and **7 end-to-end tests** per the workspace.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B) Long-lived JWT tokens with bearer auth — Sarah’s synthesis was that the developer experience can stay API-key-like while still getting JWT’s rotation and revocation capabilities.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — The gap is **no delivery confirmation mechanism**. A webhook can return **200 OK** even if the customer doesn’t actually process it, so the failure can stay silent until something like invoice reconciliation exposes it weeks later.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B) Takeshi at NovaTech in Tokyo — he was explicitly offered to review the proposal from an API consumer perspective and hasn’t been consulted yet.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace explicitly totals the four items at **~2.75 hours/week**: 1h hardcoded URLs + 45m retry logic + 1h generic error messages, plus missed bugs from the skipped test.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C) 2 prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc); Sarah's existing webhook signature module means the change is localized — about half a day.

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C) Feature 5 — it breaks even at 5 features, and becomes net positive from feature 6 onward. The workspace explicitly shows: N=5 is 25 days either way, and N=6 is where refactor is 3 days ahead.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The root cause is a spec ambiguity. The spec says “subscription start date,” but that could mean the customer’s signup date (Jan 15) or the billing period start (Feb 1). Engineering built the latter, so this is a 1-line UI label/wording issue, not a backend or data bug.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text:
B) Annual billing requires proration, refund, and dunning changes that would conflict with the March 31 billing migration launch.

The workspace shows Billing Migration Phase 2 is already on a tight March 31 deadline with no buffer, and it’s blocked on webhook format/reconciliation. Adding annual billing would pull the team into additional billing work right in the same window.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B) 12 minutes detection; root cause was a process gap — no testing against production-scale data with historical records. The incident log says Sarah’s alert fired at 09:12 after the 09:00 start, and the root cause was the script not being tested against production-scale data with legacy records missing the expected field.

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text:
C) 95% utilization at baseline; during typical 1.5-2x spikes the combined 143-190 req/sec exceeds the 100 limit, causing 429 errors

Baseline combined usage is 60 + 35 = 95 req/sec, which is 95% of the 100 req/sec limit. With 1.5–2x traffic spikes, that becomes 143–190 req/sec, which exceeds the limit and will trigger 429 errors.
